title: Reproducible Repository, Toolchain, and Dependency Stewardship · Decision Retrieval
summary: A delayed review reconstructs reproducible repository, toolchain, and dependency stewardship from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for reproducible healthy project repository after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation.
Pin supported runtime, package manager, compiler, formatter, linter, type checker, test runner, and build behavior with compatibility evidence.
Evaluate dependency need, maintenance, provenance, license, advisories, transitive cost, API stability, and replacement or removal path.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Repository Contract · changed case · Review Repository Contract
steps.instruction:
review case — A promising product works only in a warm local directory with floating dependencies, an exposed sample token, unclear license, and no recovery anchor. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation.

Which action demonstrates Repository Contract without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Repository Contract · changed case · Review Repository Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation. For Repository Contract · changed case · Review Repository Contract, keep Repository Contract connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A promising product works only in a warm local directory with floating dependencies, an exposed sample token, unclear license, and no recovery anchor. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Repository Contract condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NIST Secure Software Development Framework — Controls prepared organizations, protected software, well-secured production, vulnerability response, provenance, verification, and release evidence.

The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation.

For Repository Contract · changed case · Review Repository Contract, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A README installation snippet is a complete repository contract.

options.text: Establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for reproducible healthy project repository.
A README installation snippet is a complete repository contract. That claim fails the repository contract · changed case evidence boundary for Repository Contract.

For repository contract · changed case, apply Repository Contract without its stated preconditions, omit the reproducible healthy project repository trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Repository Contract for reproducible healthy project repository.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prove clone-to-gates-to-build behavior in a clean environment and record exact revision, lock, tool, artifact, and failure evidence.
